rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KATHRYN C. QVALE MARITAL TRUST DATED 1-31-2006
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Miles Jeffrey Qvales Motion To Compel Further Documents From Manatt, Phelps Phillips Llp And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Jeff's Motion to Compel Further Documents from Manatt and for Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. Jeff has not shown a waiver of the work product privilege by Manatt that would entitle him to disclosure of the redacted documents in the First Production. With respect to the Second Production, an order compelling those documents is unwarranted because Manatt properly utilized the clawback procedure under CCP Section 2031.285. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. on 1/20/2015.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KATHRYN C. QVALE MARITAL TRUST DATED 1-31-2006
rulings.Calendar Matter: Bruce H. Qvale And Laura Hiuraâ¿¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To First Set Of Requests For Admission And First Set Of Form Interrogatories Served On Miles Jeffrey Qvale
rulings.Rulings: Bruce and Laura's Motion to Compel Responses to First Set of Requests for Admission and First Set of Form Interrogatories is GRANTED. Jeff is ordered to provide complete, substantive responses to the RFAs and Form Interrogatory 17.1 within two weeks of the date of the Order. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. on 1/20/2015.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KATHRYN C. QVALE MARITAL TRUST DATED 1-31-2006
rulings.Calendar Matter: Miles Jeffrey Qvale'S Motion Contesting Manatt, Phelps & Phillps, Llc'S Notice Of Inadvertent Disclosure Of Electronically Stored Information Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2031.285(D)
rulings.Rulings: Jeff's Motion Contesting Manatt Phelps & Phillips, LLC's Notice of Inadvertent Disclosure of Electronically Stored Informoation (ESI) is DENIED. Manatt acted diligently in notifying the parties of the inadvertent disclosure and asking them to cease review of the documents and properly usted the clawback procedure under CCP 2031.285. There is no evidence that the disclosure of privileged documents was intentional and thus no waiver of the privilege occurred. The prevailing party shall submit a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling shall notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 1/20/2015, at 4:00 p.m.
